text.title: Wireframing — Examples and Antipatterns
text.summary: Low and medium fidelity wireframe patterns and antipatterns for common UI structures.

section.title: Common Element Patterns
section.p: Navigation: [Logo] [Link] [Link] [Link] [CTA] — horizontal bar with logo left, nav center, primary action right.
Hero section: [Headline] / [Subtext] / [Primary CTA Button] — three-element stack, clear visual hierarchy by size.
Card: bordered rectangle with [Image area] / Title / Description / [Action] — four zones, top-to-bottom reading order.
Form: Label above [Input field] pairs, grouped in sections, with [Submit Button] at bottom — labels above inputs, not inline.
Footer: [Link groups] | [Social icons] | [Copyright and legal] — three horizontal zones.

antipattern.description: Adding color to wireframes — even "just one accent color" for the primary button.
antipattern.reason: Any color redirects stakeholder feedback from structure to aesthetics. A single colored button generates 20 minutes of color discussion in a review that should be about layout.

antipattern.description: Wireframing only the default state — no empty, error, or loading states documented.
antipattern.reason: Missing states are discovered during development; developers implement default-only behavior and edge cases become bugs. State coverage must be explicit in the wireframe deliverable.

antipattern.description: No annotations — wireframe boxes without any behavior documentation.
antipattern.reason: An unannotated wireframe is a Rorschach test. Every developer and stakeholder interprets element behavior differently; the disagreement surfaces in QA, not in review.

antipattern.description: Jumping to a tool before sketching — opening Figma as the first step.
antipattern.reason: Design tools impose grids, component libraries, and frame structures that commit the designer to a layout direction before sufficient exploration. Paper sketches are faster and cheaper to discard.

antipattern.description: Using Lorem Ipsum placeholder text instead of real content volume estimates.
antipattern.reason: Lorem Ipsum hides information density problems. A navigation that fits "Home / About / Products" breaks with "Enterprise Resource Planning Module Configuration".

antipattern.description: Creating desktop wireframes only and noting "mobile TBD."
antipattern.reason: Mobile layouts require different hierarchy decisions than desktop — they cannot be derived from desktop wireframes without explicit design work. Mobile is a separate deliverable, not a footnote.

section.title: Fidelity Progression
section.p: Low-fidelity: boxes and lines, quick exploration, multiple concepts, pencil and paper or sticky notes. Cheap to discard. Use for early ideation before stakeholder alignment.
Medium-fidelity: basic content labels, rough layout, no visual design. Use for structure validation and early stakeholder review.
High-fidelity: detailed layout with all element positions, complete annotations, all states documented. Use as the final deliverable before visual design begins.
